response: Ensemble modeling boosts forecast skill by running many simulations. This approach shows probability of events like a hurricane hitting land. Instead of one path, we see many possible tracks. Data assimilation feeds current observations into each run. Small errors in start points grow over time. By comparing all runs, forecasters spot likely outcomes. If most models show rain, confidence rises. If they differ, uncertainty is high. This method handles chaos well. It helps emergency teams prepare for storms. A single run might miss key shifts. Many runs cover more ground. The spread tells us how sure we can be. This is vital for safety. We use this to warn people early. The system reduces false alarms. It also cuts missed warnings. Forecasters trust these results more. The technique is now standard.
